docs.title: OmniWorkboard
docs.text:
Project management workboard for AI agents.

OmniWorkboard provides a kanban-style workboard that AI agents can use to track tasks, manage projects, and coordinate work.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Kanban Board - Organize tasks across columns (Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Review, Done)
  • Priority System - Mark tasks as Low, Normal, High, or Critical priority
  • Dependencies - Define task dependencies and track blockers
  • Labels - Categorize tasks with custom labels
  • Agent Skills - Built-in skill implementation for AI agent integration
  • Persistence - Store boards using kvs.Store backend

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"

    "github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Create a board
    board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
        Name:        "Sprint 42",
        Description: "Q3 Feature Development",
    })

    // Create cards
    card1, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Implement login", "Add OAuth2 login flow", omniworkboard.PriorityHigh)
    card2, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Add tests", "Unit tests for auth module", omniworkboard.PriorityNormal)

    // Set dependency
    board.AddDependency(ctx, card2.ID, card1.ID) // card2 depends on card1

    // Move card through workflow
    board.MoveCard(ctx, card1.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)

    // List cards
    cards := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
        Column: omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
    })

    for _, c := range cards {
        fmt.Printf("%s: %s [%s]\n", c.ID[:8], c.Title, c.Column)
    }
}

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text: Use Case Description Agent Task Tracking Track tasks an AI agent is working on Project Planning Plan and organize project work Sprint Management Manage agile sprints with backlogs Personal Tasks Simple personal task management Multi-Agent Coordination Coordinate work across multiple agents

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text:
github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard
├── board.go     # Board struct and operations
├── card.go      # Card struct and operations
├── column.go    # Column types and transitions
├── tools.go     # Tool definitions for agents
├── skill.go     # Skill implementation for omniagent
└── doc.go       # Package documentation

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard@latest

docs.title: Integration with OmniAgent
docs.text:
OmniWorkboard provides a compiled skill for seamless integration:

import (
    "github.com/plexusone/omniagent/agent"
    "github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard"
)

// Create workboard skill
skill := omniworkboard.NewSkill()

// Add to agent
a, err := agent.New(config,
    agent.WithCompiledSkill(skill),
)

Once integrated, the agent can use workboard tools:

User: Create a card for "Fix login bug" with high priority
Agent: [calls create_card] Created card "Fix login bug" in Backlog with high priority.

User: Move it to In Progress
Agent: [calls move_card] Moved "Fix login bug" to In Progress.

User: What's on my board?
Agent: [calls list_cards] You have 3 cards:
- In Progress: Fix login bug (High)
- Todo: Add unit tests (Normal)
- Backlog: Update documentation (Low)

docs.title: Agent Integration
docs.text: OmniWorkboard provides a compiled skill for seamless integration with OmniAgent and other AI agent frameworks.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
import (
    "github.com/plexusone/omniagent/agent"
    "github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard"
)

// Create skill
skill := omniworkboard.NewSkill()

// Add to agent
a, err := agent.New(config,
    agent.WithCompiledSkill(skill),
)

docs.title: Available Tools
docs.text: The workboard skill provides these tools to the agent:

docs.title: create_card
docs.text:
Create a new task card.

Parameter Type Required Description title string Yes Card title description string No Card description priority string No low, normal, high, critical labels array No List of labels

Example: 

User: Create a high priority card for "Fix authentication bug"
Agent: [calls create_card with title="Fix authentication bug", priority="high"]
       Created card "Fix authentication bug" in Backlog with high priority (ID: abc123)

docs.title: move_card
docs.text:
Move a card to a different column.

Parameter Type Required Description card_id string Yes Card ID column string Yes Target column

Example: 

User: Move the auth bug card to In Progress
Agent: [calls move_card with card_id="abc123", column="in_progress"]
       Moved "Fix authentication bug" to In Progress

docs.title: list_cards
docs.text:
List cards with optional filters.

Parameter Type Required Description column string No Filter by column priority string No Filter by priority label string No Filter by label

Example: 

User: What's in progress?
Agent: [calls list_cards with column="in_progress"]
       In Progress:
       1. Fix authentication bug (High) - abc123
       2. Update API endpoints (Normal) - def456

docs.title: update_card
docs.text:
Update card details.

Parameter Type Required Description card_id string Yes Card ID title string No New title description string No New description priority string No New priority labels array No New labels

docs.title: add_dependency
docs.text:
Add a dependency between cards.

Parameter Type Required Description card_id string Yes Card that depends depends_on string Yes Card that blocks

Example: 

User: The tests card depends on the login card
Agent: [calls add_dependency with card_id="tests123", depends_on="login456"]
       Added dependency: "Write tests" now depends on "Implement login"

docs.title: get_board
docs.text:
Get the current board state.

No parameters required.

docs.title: Skill Configuration
docs.text:
skill := omniworkboard.NewSkill(omniworkboard.SkillConfig{
    // Board name (creates new or loads existing)
    BoardName: "My Project",

    // Board description
    BoardDescription: "Project management board",

    // Custom columns (optional)
    Columns: []omniworkboard.ColumnType{
        omniworkboard.ColumnTodo,
        omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
        omniworkboard.ColumnDone,
    },
})

docs.title: Storage Integration
docs.text:
The skill implements compiled.StorageAware for automatic storage injection:

import (
    "github.com/plexusone/omnistorage-core/kvs"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnistorage-core/sqlite"
)

// Create storage
backend, _ := sqlite.Open("agent.db")
store := kvs.New(backend)

// Agent with storage
a, err := agent.New(config,
    agent.WithStorage(store),
    agent.WithCompiledSkill(omniworkboard.NewSkill()),
)
// Skill automatically receives storage

docs.title: Project Setup
docs.text:
User: Let's set up a sprint board. Create cards for:
      1. Design API schema (high priority)
      2. Implement endpoints
      3. Write tests
      4. Deploy to staging

Agent: I'll create those cards for you.

[creates 4 cards]

Created 4 cards in Backlog:
- Design API schema (High) - abc123
- Implement endpoints (Normal) - def456
- Write tests (Normal) - ghi789
- Deploy to staging (Normal) - jkl012

User: The endpoints depend on the design, tests depend on endpoints,
      and deploy depends on tests.

Agent: [adds 3 dependencies]

Dependencies set:
- "Implement endpoints" depends on "Design API schema"
- "Write tests" depends on "Implement endpoints"
- "Deploy to staging" depends on "Write tests"

docs.title: Daily Standup
docs.text:
User: What's my board status?

Agent: [calls list_cards]

Here's your board status:

Done (2):
- Design API schema (High)
- Implement endpoints (Normal)

In Progress (1):
- Write tests (Normal)

Blocked (1):
- Deploy to staging - blocked by "Write tests"

Backlog (0): empty

docs.title: Moving Through Workflow
docs.text:
User: I finished writing tests, move it to review

Agent: [calls move_card with column="review"]

Moved "Write tests" to Review.

Note: "Deploy to staging" is now unblocked and ready to start.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The skill handles common errors gracefully:

User: Move card xyz123 to done

Agent: I couldn't find a card with ID "xyz123". Would you like me to
       list all cards so you can identify the correct one?

User: Move the deploy card to done

Agent: I can't move "Deploy to staging" to Done because it's blocked
       by "Write tests" which is still in Review. Would you like me
       to move "Write tests" to Done first?

docs.title: Clear Task Descriptions
docs.text:
User: Create a card for "login"  # Too vague

User: Create a high priority card for "Implement OAuth2 login with
      Google and GitHub providers" with labels "auth" and "feature"

docs.title: Use Dependencies
docs.text:
User: Make sure the testing card can't be done until the
      implementation is complete

Agent: [adds dependency]
       "Testing" now depends on "Implementation"

docs.title: Regular Updates
docs.text:
User: What's blocking progress?

Agent: [analyzes dependencies]
       2 cards are blocked:
       - "Deployment" is blocked by "Testing" (in Review)
       - "Documentation" is blocked by "API Design" (in Progress)

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks through setting up OmniWorkboard for project management.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard@latest

docs.title: Creating a Board
docs.text:
import "github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard"

// Create with default columns
board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name:        "My Project",
    Description: "Project description",
})

// Create with custom columns
board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name: "Simple Board",
    Columns: []omniworkboard.ColumnType{
        omniworkboard.ColumnTodo,
        omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
        omniworkboard.ColumnDone,
    },
})

docs.title: Creating Cards
docs.text:
ctx := context.Background()

// Create a high-priority card
card, err := board.CreateCard(ctx,
    "Implement feature X",           // Title
    "Detailed description here",      // Description
    omniworkboard.PriorityHigh,       // Priority
)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

fmt.Printf("Created card: %s\n", card.ID)

docs.title: Moving Cards
docs.text:
// Move to In Progress
err := board.MoveCard(ctx, card.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Move to Done
err = board.MoveCard(ctx, card.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnDone)

docs.title: Listing Cards
docs.text:
// List all cards
cards := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{})

// List by column
inProgress := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Column: omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
})

// List by priority
highPriority := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Priority: omniworkboard.PriorityHigh,
})

// List by label
bugFixes := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Label: "bug",
})

docs.title: Managing Dependencies
docs.text:
// Create cards
taskA, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Task A", "", omniworkboard.PriorityNormal)
taskB, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Task B", "", omniworkboard.PriorityNormal)

// Task B depends on Task A (must complete A before B)
err := board.AddDependency(ctx, taskB.ID, taskA.ID)

// Check if card is blocked
card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, taskB.ID)
if len(card.BlockedBy) > 0 {
    fmt.Println("Task B is blocked by:", card.BlockedBy)
}

docs.title: Workflow Example
docs.text:
A typical workflow:

ctx := context.Background()

// 1. Create board
board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name: "Sprint 42",
})

// 2. Add cards to backlog
login, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Implement OAuth login", "", omniworkboard.PriorityHigh)
tests, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Write auth tests", "", omniworkboard.PriorityNormal)
docs, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Update API docs", "", omniworkboard.PriorityLow)

// 3. Set dependencies
board.AddDependency(ctx, tests.ID, login.ID)  // tests need login first
board.AddDependency(ctx, docs.ID, tests.ID)   // docs need tests first

// 4. Start sprint - move to todo
board.MoveCard(ctx, login.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnTodo)

// 5. Work on login
board.MoveCard(ctx, login.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)
// ... do work ...
board.MoveCard(ctx, login.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnReview)
// ... review passes ...
board.MoveCard(ctx, login.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnDone)

// 6. tests is now unblocked, move to todo
board.MoveCard(ctx, tests.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnTodo)

// 7. Check remaining work
remaining := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{})
for _, c := range remaining {
    if c.Column != omniworkboard.ColumnDone {
        fmt.Printf("Remaining: %s [%s]\n", c.Title, c.Column)
    }
}

docs.title: Using kvs.Store
docs.text:
import (
    "github.com/plexusone/omnistorage-core/kvs"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnistorage-core/sqlite"
)

// Create storage
backend, _ := sqlite.Open("workboard.db")
store := kvs.New(backend)

// Create skill with storage
skill := omniworkboard.NewSkill()
skill.SetStorage(store)

docs.title: Manual Save/Load
docs.text:
// Save board state
data, _ := json.Marshal(board)
store.Set(ctx, "board:sprint-42", data)

// Load board state
data, _ := store.Get(ctx, "board:sprint-42")
var board omniworkboard.Board
json.Unmarshal(data, &board)

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page provides an overview of the main types and functions in OmniWorkboard.

docs.title: Board
docs.text:
type Board struct {
    ID          string            `json:"id"`
    Name        string            `json:"name"`
    Description string            `json:"description,omitempty"`
    Columns     []ColumnType      `json:"columns"`
    Cards       map[string]*Card  `json:"cards"`
    CreatedAt   time.Time         `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt   time.Time         `json:"updated_at"`
}

docs.title: Card
docs.text:
type Card struct {
    ID          string            `json:"id"`
    Title       string            `json:"title"`
    Description string            `json:"description"`
    Column      ColumnType        `json:"column"`
    Priority    Priority          `json:"priority"`
    DependsOn   []string          `json:"depends_on"`
    BlockedBy   []string          `json:"blocked_by"`
    Labels      []string          `json:"labels"`
    Metadata    map[string]any    `json:"metadata"`
    CreatedAt   time.Time         `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt   time.Time         `json:"updated_at"`
    CompletedAt *time.Time        `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
}

docs.title: ColumnType
docs.text:
type ColumnType string

const (
    ColumnBacklog    ColumnType = "backlog"
    ColumnTodo       ColumnType = "todo"
    ColumnInProgress ColumnType = "in_progress"
    ColumnReview     ColumnType = "review"
    ColumnDone       ColumnType = "done"
)

docs.title: Priority
docs.text:
type Priority int

const (
    PriorityLow      Priority = 0
    PriorityNormal   Priority = 1
    PriorityHigh     Priority = 2
    PriorityCritical Priority = 3
)

docs.title: NewBoard
docs.text:
func NewBoard(cfg BoardConfig) *Board

Creates a new workboard with the given configuration.

docs.title: Board Methods
docs.text:
func (b *Board) CreateCard(ctx context.Context, title, description string, priority Priority) (*Card, error)
func (b *Board) GetCard(ctx context.Context, id string) (*Card, error)
func (b *Board) UpdateCard(ctx context.Context, id string, opts UpdateCardOptions) error
func (b *Board) DeleteCard(ctx context.Context, id string) error
func (b *Board) MoveCard(ctx context.Context, id string, column ColumnType) error
func (b *Board) ListCards(ctx context.Context, opts ListOptions) []*Card
func (b *Board) AddDependency(ctx context.Context, cardID, dependsOnID string) error
func (b *Board) RemoveDependency(ctx context.Context, cardID, dependsOnID string) error
func (b *Board) AddLabel(ctx context.Context, cardID, label string) error
func (b *Board) RemoveLabel(ctx context.Context, cardID, label string) error

docs.title: BoardConfig
docs.text:
type BoardConfig struct {
    Name        string
    Description string
    Columns     []ColumnType
}

docs.title: UpdateCardOptions
docs.text:
type UpdateCardOptions struct {
    Title       string
    Description string
    Priority    Priority
    Labels      []string
}

docs.title: ListOptions
docs.text:
type ListOptions struct {
    Column      ColumnType
    Priority    Priority
    Label       string
    BlockedOnly bool
}

docs.title: Skill
docs.text:
type Skill struct {
    // implements compiled.Skill
}

func NewSkill() *Skill
func (s *Skill) Name() string
func (s *Skill) Description() string
func (s *Skill) Tools() []skill.Tool
func (s *Skill) Init(ctx context.Context) error
func (s *Skill) Close() error

docs.title: WorkboardTools
docs.text:
type WorkboardTools struct {
    board *Board
}

func NewWorkboardTools(board *Board) *WorkboardTools
func (t *WorkboardTools) CreateCard(ctx context.Context, params map[string]any) (any, error)

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
var (
    ErrCardNotFound    = errors.New("card not found")
    ErrCycleDetected   = errors.New("dependency would create cycle")
    ErrSelfDependency  = errors.New("card cannot depend on itself")
    ErrInvalidColumn   = errors.New("invalid column")
    ErrInvalidPriority = errors.New("invalid priority")
)

Returns the default column order: Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Review, Done.

docs.title: Full Documentation
docs.text: For complete API documentation, see pkg.go.dev/github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard.

docs.title: Boards
docs.text: A board is the top-level container that organizes cards into columns.

docs.title: Default Configuration
docs.text:
board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name: "My Project",
})

Default columns: Backlog → Todo → In Progress → Review → Done

docs.title: Custom Columns
docs.text:
board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name: "Simple Board",
    Columns: []omniworkboard.ColumnType{
        omniworkboard.ColumnTodo,
        omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
        omniworkboard.ColumnDone,
    },
})

docs.title: Board Structure
docs.text:
type Board struct {
    ID          string            `json:"id"`
    Name        string            `json:"name"`
    Description string            `json:"description,omitempty"`
    Columns     []ColumnType      `json:"columns"`
    Cards       map[string]*Card  `json:"cards"`
    CreatedAt   time.Time         `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt   time.Time         `json:"updated_at"`
}

docs.title: Column Types
docs.text:
const (
    ColumnBacklog    ColumnType = "backlog"
    ColumnTodo       ColumnType = "todo"
    ColumnInProgress ColumnType = "in_progress"
    ColumnReview     ColumnType = "review"
    ColumnDone       ColumnType = "done"
)

docs.title: Default Columns
docs.text:
func DefaultColumns() []ColumnType {
    return []ColumnType{
        ColumnBacklog,
        ColumnTodo,
        ColumnInProgress,
        ColumnReview,
        ColumnDone,
    }
}

docs.title: Get Board Info
docs.text:
fmt.Printf("Board: %s\n", board.Name)
fmt.Printf("Columns: %v\n", board.Columns)
fmt.Printf("Cards: %d\n", len(board.Cards))

docs.title: Get Cards by Column
docs.text:
cards := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Column: omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
})

docs.title: Board Statistics
docs.text:
stats := board.Stats(ctx)
fmt.Printf("Total cards: %d\n", stats.Total)
fmt.Printf("In Progress: %d\n", stats.ByColumn[omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress])
fmt.Printf("Blocked: %d\n", stats.Blocked)

docs.title: Valid Transitions
docs.text:
Cards can move to any column, but typical workflow is:

Backlog → Todo → In Progress → Review → Done

docs.title: Skip Columns
docs.text:
Cards can skip columns if needed:

// Direct to In Progress
board.MoveCard(ctx, cardID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)

// Direct to Done (e.g., cancelled)
board.MoveCard(ctx, cardID, omniworkboard.ColumnDone)

docs.title: Move Backwards
docs.text:
Cards can move backwards:

// Failed review, back to In Progress
board.MoveCard(ctx, cardID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)

docs.title: Multiple Boards
docs.text:
For multiple projects, create separate boards:

sprint42 := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name: "Sprint 42",
})

sprint43 := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name: "Sprint 43",
})

docs.title: JSON Export
docs.text:
data, err := json.Marshal(board)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
// Store or transmit data

docs.title: JSON Import
docs.text:
var board omniworkboard.Board
err := json.Unmarshal(data, &board)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

docs.title: Board Naming
docs.text:
Use clear, descriptive names:

// Good
"Sprint 42 - Q3 Features"
"Bug Triage - June 2026"
"Project Alpha - Phase 1"

// Less clear
"Board 1"
"My Tasks"

docs.title: Column Selection
docs.text:
Match columns to your workflow:

Workflow Columns Simple Todo, In Progress, Done Agile Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Review, Done Support New, Triaging, Investigating, Resolved Content Draft, Review, Editing, Published

docs.title: Board Size
docs.text:
Keep boards focused:

  • One board per project/sprint
  • Archive completed boards
  • Split large boards if > 50 active cards

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Cards - Card operations
  • Dependencies - Task dependencies

docs.title: Cards
docs.text: Cards represent individual tasks or work items on a board.

docs.title: Card Structure
docs.text:
type Card struct {
    ID          string            `json:"id"`
    Title       string            `json:"title"`
    Description string            `json:"description"`
    Column      ColumnType        `json:"column"`
    Priority    Priority          `json:"priority"`
    DependsOn   []string          `json:"depends_on"`
    BlockedBy   []string          `json:"blocked_by"`
    Labels      []string          `json:"labels"`
    Metadata    map[string]any    `json:"metadata"`
    CreatedAt   time.Time         `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt   time.Time         `json:"updated_at"`
    CompletedAt *time.Time        `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
}

docs.title: Basic Card
docs.text:
card, err := board.CreateCard(ctx,
    "Fix login bug",               // Title
    "Users can't login with SSO",  // Description
    omniworkboard.PriorityHigh,    // Priority
)

docs.title: Card with Options
docs.text:
card, err := board.CreateCardWithOptions(ctx, omniworkboard.CreateCardOptions{
    Title:       "Implement feature",
    Description: "Add new feature X",
    Priority:    omniworkboard.PriorityNormal,
    Labels:      []string{"feature", "backend"},
    Metadata: map[string]any{
        "estimate": "3d",
        "assignee": "alice",
    },
})

docs.title: Priority Levels
docs.text:
const (
    PriorityLow      Priority = 0
    PriorityNormal   Priority = 1
    PriorityHigh     Priority = 2
    PriorityCritical Priority = 3
)

docs.title: Priority Guidelines
docs.text: Priority Response Time Examples Critical Immediate Production down, security issue High Same day Important feature, blocking bug Normal This sprint Regular work items Low When possible Tech debt, nice-to-haves

docs.title: Get Card
docs.text:
card, err := board.GetCard(ctx, cardID)
if err != nil {
    // Card not found
}

docs.title: Update Card
docs.text:
err := board.UpdateCard(ctx, cardID, omniworkboard.UpdateCardOptions{
    Title:       "Updated title",
    Description: "Updated description",
    Priority:    omniworkboard.PriorityCritical,
})

docs.title: Move Card
docs.text: err := board.MoveCard(ctx, cardID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)

docs.title: Delete Card
docs.text: err := board.DeleteCard(ctx, cardID)

docs.title: Add Labels
docs.text:
err := board.AddLabel(ctx, cardID, "urgent")
err = board.AddLabel(ctx, cardID, "backend")

docs.title: Remove Labels
docs.text: err := board.RemoveLabel(ctx, cardID, "urgent")

docs.title: All Cards
docs.text: cards := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{})

docs.title: By Column
docs.text:
inProgress := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Column: omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
})

docs.title: By Priority
docs.text:
urgent := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Priority: omniworkboard.PriorityCritical,
})

docs.title: By Label
docs.text:
bugs := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Label: "bug",
})

docs.title: Blocked Cards
docs.text:
blocked := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    BlockedOnly: true,
})

docs.title: Multiple Filters
docs.text:
// High priority bugs in progress
cards := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    Column:   omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress,
    Priority: omniworkboard.PriorityHigh,
    Label:    "bug",
})

docs.title: Card Metadata
docs.text:
Store additional information in metadata:

card.Metadata = map[string]any{
    "estimate":    "2d",
    "assignee":    "bob",
    "sprint":      42,
    "story_points": 5,
    "due_date":    "2026-06-15",
    "links": []string{
        "https://github.com/org/repo/issues/123",
    },
}

docs.title: Accessing Metadata
docs.text:
if assignee, ok := card.Metadata["assignee"].(string); ok {
    fmt.Printf("Assigned to: %s\n", assignee)
}

docs.title: Marking Complete
docs.text:
When a card moves to Done, it's automatically timestamped:

board.MoveCard(ctx, cardID, omniworkboard.ColumnDone)

card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardID)
fmt.Printf("Completed at: %v\n", card.CompletedAt)

docs.title: Moving Back
docs.text:
If moved back from Done, CompletedAt is cleared:

board.MoveCard(ctx, cardID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)

card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardID)
// card.CompletedAt is nil

docs.title: Sorting
docs.text:
Cards are sorted by:

  1. Priority (Critical first)
  2. Created date (oldest first)
cards := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{})
// Already sorted by priority, then creation time

docs.title: Clear Titles
docs.text:
// Good - specific and actionable
"Fix SSO login timeout error"
"Add pagination to user list API"
"Update Docker base image to Alpine 3.18"

// Bad - vague
"Fix bug"
"Update stuff"
"Work on feature"

docs.title: Useful Descriptions
docs.text:
Include:

  • What needs to be done
  • Why it's needed
  • Acceptance criteria
  • Links to related resources
card, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx,
    "Add rate limiting to API",
    `## Description
Implement rate limiting to prevent abuse.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100 requests per minute per user
- [ ] Return 429 status when exceeded
- [ ] Add X-RateLimit headers

## Links
- Design doc: https://...
- Issue: #123`,
    omniworkboard.PriorityHigh,
)

docs.title: Label Conventions
docs.text:
Establish consistent labels:

Types: bug, feature, enhancement, docs, chore
Areas: frontend, backend, api, database, infra
Priority: p0, p1, p2, p3
Status: blocked, needs-review, ready

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Boards - Board operations
  • Dependencies - Managing dependencies
  • Overview - Architecture overview

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Dependencies model "must complete before" relationships between cards.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
When Card B depends on Card A:

  • Card B is "blocked by" Card A
  • Card A is "blocking" Card B
  • Card B cannot be considered complete until Card A is Done
Card A (Login)
    │
    │ blocks
    ▼
Card B (Tests) ← blocked until Card A is Done
    │
    │ blocks
    ▼
Card C (Deploy) ← blocked until Card B is Done

docs.title: Adding Dependencies
docs.text:
// Card B depends on Card A
err := board.AddDependency(ctx, cardB.ID, cardA.ID)

// Multiple dependencies
err = board.AddDependency(ctx, cardC.ID, cardA.ID)
err = board.AddDependency(ctx, cardC.ID, cardB.ID)

docs.title: Removing Dependencies
docs.text: err := board.RemoveDependency(ctx, cardB.ID, cardA.ID)

docs.title: DependsOn vs BlockedBy
docs.text:
card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardID)

// DependsOn - cards this card waits for
fmt.Printf("Depends on: %v\n", card.DependsOn)

// BlockedBy - cards that are blocking (not Done)
fmt.Printf("Blocked by: %v\n", card.BlockedBy)

Key difference:

  • DependsOn - All declared dependencies
  • BlockedBy - Dependencies that are not yet Done

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
// Card A is Done
// Card B (depends on A, C) - A is Done, C is not
card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardB.ID)

card.DependsOn  // ["cardA", "cardC"]
card.BlockedBy  // ["cardC"] - only C is blocking

docs.title: List Blocked Cards
docs.text:
blocked := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    BlockedOnly: true,
})

for _, c := range blocked {
    fmt.Printf("%s blocked by: %v\n", c.Title, c.BlockedBy)
}

docs.title: Check if Blocked
docs.text:
card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardID)
if len(card.BlockedBy) > 0 {
    fmt.Printf("Card is blocked by %d cards\n", len(card.BlockedBy))
}

docs.title: Dependency Chains
docs.text:
Dependencies can form chains:

// A → B → C → D
board.AddDependency(ctx, cardB.ID, cardA.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, cardC.ID, cardB.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, cardD.ID, cardC.ID)

When A completes, B unblocks. When B completes, C unblocks. And so on.

docs.title: Cycle Detection
docs.text:
Circular dependencies are prevented:

// This would create A → B → A cycle
board.AddDependency(ctx, cardA.ID, cardB.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, cardB.ID, cardA.ID) // Error: cycle detected

docs.title: Sequential Tasks
docs.text:
Design → Implement → Test → Deploy

board.AddDependency(ctx, implement.ID, design.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, test.ID, implement.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, deploy.ID, test.ID)

docs.title: Parallel with Join
docs.text:
┌─ Frontend ─┐
API ─┤            ├─ Integration
     └─ Backend ──┘

board.AddDependency(ctx, frontend.ID, api.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, backend.ID, api.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, integration.ID, frontend.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, integration.ID, backend.ID)

docs.title: Multiple Prerequisites
docs.text:
┌─ Requirement A ─┐
Task ┼─ Requirement B ─┼
     └─ Requirement C ─┘

board.AddDependency(ctx, task.ID, reqA.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, task.ID, reqB.ID)
board.AddDependency(ctx, task.ID, reqC.ID)

docs.title: Unblocking
docs.text:
When a blocking card moves to Done, dependents are automatically unblocked:

// B depends on A
board.AddDependency(ctx, cardB.ID, cardA.ID)

// B is blocked
card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardB.ID)
fmt.Println(card.BlockedBy) // ["cardA"]

// Complete A
board.MoveCard(ctx, cardA.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnDone)

// B is unblocked
card, _ = board.GetCard(ctx, cardB.ID)
fmt.Println(card.BlockedBy) // []

docs.title: Get Dependency Graph
docs.text:
graph := board.DependencyGraph(ctx)

for cardID, deps := range graph {
    card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardID)
    fmt.Printf("%s depends on:\n", card.Title)
    for _, depID := range deps {
        dep, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, depID)
        fmt.Printf("  - %s\n", dep.Title)
    }
}

docs.title: Critical Path
docs.text:
Find the longest dependency chain:

path := board.CriticalPath(ctx)
for i, cardID := range path {
    card, _ := board.GetCard(ctx, cardID)
    fmt.Printf("%d. %s\n", i+1, card.Title)
}

docs.title: Keep Chains Short
docs.text:
Long chains increase risk:

// Risky: long chain
A → B → C → D → E → F → G

// Better: parallel where possible
A → B ─┐
A → C ─┼→ G
A → D ─┘

docs.title: Explicit Dependencies
docs.text:
Only add necessary dependencies:

// Don't add if not truly dependent
// ❌ "Write tests" depends on "Update README"

// ✓ Add if there's a real dependency
// "Write tests" depends on "Implement feature"

docs.title: Review Blocked Cards
docs.text:
Regularly check blocked cards:

blocked := board.ListCards(ctx, omniworkboard.ListOptions{
    BlockedOnly: true,
})

if len(blocked) > 5 {
    fmt.Println("Warning: many blocked cards, review dependencies")
}

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
err := board.AddDependency(ctx, cardA.ID, cardB.ID)
if err != nil {
    switch {
    case errors.Is(err, omniworkboard.ErrCardNotFound):
        fmt.Println("Card not found")
    case errors.Is(err, omniworkboard.ErrCycleDetected):
        fmt.Println("Would create circular dependency")
    case errors.Is(err, omniworkboard.ErrSelfDependency):
        fmt.Println("Card cannot depend on itself")
    }
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Cards - Card operations
  • Boards - Board operations
  • Overview - Architecture overview

docs.title: Concepts Overview
docs.text: OmniWorkboard implements a kanban-style workboard with boards, columns, cards, and dependencies.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Board
├── Columns: [Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Review, Done]
└── Cards
    ├── Card 1
    │   ├── Title, Description
    │   ├── Column: In Progress
    │   ├── Priority: High
    │   ├── Labels: [bug, urgent]
    │   └── DependsOn: []
    └── Card 2
        ├── Title, Description
        ├── Column: Todo
        ├── Priority: Normal
        ├── Labels: [feature]
        └── DependsOn: [Card 1]  ← Blocked by Card 1

docs.title: Board
docs.text:
A board is a container for cards organized into columns. Each board has:

  • Name - Identifier (e.g., "Sprint 42")
  • Description - Optional details
  • Columns - Ordered list of workflow stages
  • Cards - Task items on the board

docs.title: Columns
docs.text:
Columns represent workflow stages:

Column Purpose Backlog Future work, not yet planned Todo Planned work, ready to start In Progress Currently being worked on Review Completed, awaiting review Done Finished work

docs.title: Cards
docs.text:
Cards represent individual tasks:

Field Description Title Short task name Description Detailed description Column Current workflow stage Priority Low, Normal, High, Critical Labels Categorization tags DependsOn Cards that must complete first BlockedBy Computed list of blocking cards

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
Dependencies model "must complete before" relationships:

Card A ──depends on──> Card B
        (Card A blocked until Card B is Done)

docs.title: Blocking Logic
docs.text:
1. Card B depends on Card A
  2. While Card A is not Done, Card B shows as "blocked"
  3. When Card A moves to Done, Card B becomes unblocked
  4. Card B can then proceed through the workflow

docs.title: Priority System
docs.text: Priority When to Use Critical Production issues, urgent bugs High Important features, deadlines Normal Regular work items Low Nice-to-haves, cleanup

docs.title: Labels
docs.text:
Labels categorize cards:

bug, feature, enhancement, documentation
frontend, backend, infrastructure
p0, p1, p2, p3
sprint-42, q3-release

docs.title: Thread Safety
docs.text:
All board operations are thread-safe:

// Safe for concurrent access
go board.CreateCard(ctx, ...)
go board.MoveCard(ctx, ...)
go board.ListCards(ctx, ...)

docs.title: Persistence
docs.text:
Boards can be persisted using kvs.Store:

skill := omniworkboard.NewSkill()
skill.SetStorage(store)  // Auto-saves state

docs.title: AI Agents
docs.text:
Via compiled skill:

agent.WithCompiledSkill(omniworkboard.NewSkill())

docs.title: Direct API
docs.text:
Via Go package:

board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(config)
card, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, ...)

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
Via omniagent CLI:

omniagent chat
> Create a card for "Fix bug"

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Boards - Board operations
  • Cards - Card operations
  • Dependencies - Dependency management

docs.title: v0.1.0
docs.text:
Release Date: 2026-06-10

Initial release of OmniWorkboard.

docs.title: Board Management
docs.text:
• Create boards with customizable columns
  • Default kanban columns: Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Review, Done
  • Thread-safe board operations

docs.title: Card Operations
docs.text:
• Create, read, update, delete cards
  • Move cards between columns
  • Priority levels: Low, Normal, High, Critical
  • Label support for categorization
  • Metadata storage for custom fields

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Define "depends on" relationships between cards
  • Automatic blocking/unblocking based on completion
  • Cycle detection to prevent circular dependencies
  • Compute blocked cards in real-time

docs.title: Agent Integration
docs.text:
• Compiled skill implementation for OmniAgent
  • Tools: create_card, move_card, list_cards, update_card, add_dependency, get_board
  • Storage-aware for persistence via kvs.Store

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text:
github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard
├── board.go     # Board struct and operations
├── card.go      # Card struct and operations
├── column.go    # Column types
├── tools.go     # Tool definitions
├── skill.go     # Skill implementation
└── board_test.go

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• github.com/google/uuid - UUID generation
  • github.com/plexusone/omniskill - Skill interface
  • github.com/plexusone/omnistorage-core - Storage interface

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard@v0.1.0

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
import "github.com/plexusone/omniworkboard"

// Create board
board := omniworkboard.NewBoard(omniworkboard.BoardConfig{
    Name: "My Project",
})

// Create card
card, _ := board.CreateCard(ctx, "Task", "Description", omniworkboard.PriorityNormal)

// Move card
board.MoveCard(ctx, card.ID, omniworkboard.ColumnInProgress)

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Contributors
docs.text: • PlexusOne Team
